feed.title: AO3 - Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV) (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV) on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: Love Me (Not Too Hard)
entry.summary:
Author: thatsanovelidea

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 50,490Chapters: 10/?Kudos: 165

Summary: It was a shitshow, to say the least. Despite, what he’d told Bobby all those months ago, he did not have a handle on the problem. He had the opposite. He had zero handles, and he was too embarrassed to bring it up. It was definitely an issue, but it wasn’t too serious. And it didn’t affect anybody besides himself. But it was definitely affecting him in a bad way. Hard day at work? Go have sex to feel better. Bobby yell at him for screwing up again? Find an old guy to tell him he’s good for something. See a happy family on a call and remember he hasn’t spoken to Maddie in seven years? Blackout so he doesn’t have to be reminded of that gaping wound. It was really bad. Yeah. But like, privately bad. Secretly bad. Except for his roommates, who were starting to get pissed about him stumbling around drunk with his guests. So, he started to look for a new apartment in earnest. And just when he was starting to think things were getting actually concerning, and pretty soon he wouldn’t even remember what the point of anything was, who walked into the fire station, looking perfect and attractive and ready to change Buck’s life? Eddie fucking Diaz.   or: Buck's sex addiction taken seriously.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: How to Accidentally Confess Feelings in Front of Your Coworkers
entry.summary:
Author: goldenbuck118

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,226Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 1

Summary: The 118 decides to play a “Newlywed Game” style challenge during shift. Unfortunately for everyone involved, Buck and Eddie know each other with genuinely horrifying accuracy. Or thirty questions, escalating emotional devastation, one very public realization that Buck and Eddie might already be halfway married.

entry.title: miles to go and promises to keep
entry.summary:
Author: sinnabonka

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 18,395Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 0

Summary: They eat. They talk. They laugh. It’s all the same, but the picture they assemble out of the pieces this time turns out different.Eddie smiles around a mouthful of his burger, his eyes not leaving Buck’s face for a second. Buck’s knee is pressed warm between his own, while his other leg, the one that never really forgot the crushing weight of the ladder truck, is stretched out under the table, claiming space, unapologetic.Buck catches a drop of sauce sliding down his chin and licks it off his finger, not breaking eye contact. Eddie has that sudden, vivid urge to grab Buck’s wrist, pull his hand to his mouth, clean it for him instead – and the thought itself isn’t what surprises him. It’s the fact that for the first time in his life it doesn’t send him scrambling for cover. It just… sits there. Simple. Allowed.Or,Life finally hands you everything you ever wanted – start looking for a catch.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Misadventures in babysitting
entry.summary:
Author: goldenbuck118

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,466Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 8

Summary: Buck and Eddie insist they can handle babysitting Jee-Yun alone for one afternoon. They are firefighters. Paramedics. First responders. They have survived tsunamis, lawsuits, sniper attacks, lightning strikes, and Los Angeles traffic. Surely one toddler can’t be that hard. Unfortunately for them, Jee-Yun Han is three years old, emotionally unpredictable, sticky for reasons no one can identify, and apparently powered by nuclear energy. By hour two, Buck’s socks are wet, Eddie’s reconsidering every life choice he’s ever made, and Chimney is enjoying their suffering far too much. Or: Buck and Eddie accidentally play house with Jee-Yun for a day and Eddie realizes several emotionally devastating things about himself in the process.

entry.title: Fault Line
entry.summary:
Author: fancurling

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,694Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 10

Summary: Eddie is fine with Buck dating someone new. He is. Mostly. But when an earthquake tears through Los Angeles and Buck disappears beneath the wreckage of a collapsed hotel, Eddie realizes jealousy was never the real problem. The problem was that Buck had become home long before Eddie knew how to say it.

entry.summary:
Author: charmingqueenie

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 71,173Chapters: 13/13Kudos: 72

Summary: Eddie leans back, gesturing for Bobby to get started. Bobby sighs, pulling off his glasses. "So how did you… become this?" Eddie cocks his head. "You can say the word." "I- uh." Bobby clears his throat. "How did you become a vampire?" or IWTV Buddie AU

entry.title: i miss it, i miss you
entry.summary:
Author: louisloml28

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,404Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 21

Summary: “I just can’t stop thinking about everything he had to deal with—everything I put on him. Bobby would know what to do. He always knew,” Buck shouts, voice cracking.Eddie cups Buck’s face, steady and firm. “Buck… you helped raise my son. Look at Chris. You helped shape the man he’s becoming.”ORBuck has been struggling with Theo, and all he can think about is how Bobby would have loved him.

entry.title: Bee Happy
entry.summary:
Author: MsVortex

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,120Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 28

Summary: The night of her mothers funeral Shannon enters a crowded bar, looking for connection. She finds it in a handsome man, about her age, with an unusual birthmark above his eye. Shannon doesn't recognize the significance of that night until it's too late and she is looking down on the second pink line of a pregnancy test, feeling the worst kind of déjà vu. Things get complicated when she confronts her husband at his new firehouse only to come face-to-face with his colleague and her son's new best friend.

entry.title: sinking (in everything that follows)
entry.summary:
Author: nocturalApollo

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,379Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 18

Summary: Christopher still needs his dad. However foggy his current memory is, he can still picture that bashful grin and those red glasses. He can’t let that kid down again, no matter how against the idea Eddie may be, it’s better Buck risks his life than risk Bonnie hurting his husband. If anything were to happen to him, Christopher would have no one left. He doubts Bonnie and Earl would let him go if it came to killing Eddie. He has to try. ------------------ Day 26: “I didn’t think I’d get out of there alive”Broken lock | successful escape | abandoned mine

entry.title: I should have worshipped him sooner
entry.summary:
Author: thegreaterdivide

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,893Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 41

Summary: Buck can’t look away. It's a sick joke, how unnatural, how horrible the sight is, but he can’t bring himself to look away. He thinks he pushes Harry out of the way, needing a better view. Shoves Ravi aside to stand in the front row. A barricade spot for death. Buck almost laughs at the sight. He feels it bubbling up his throat, threatening to explode out of him. He wants to laugh in some weird, delirious relief because that can’t be Eddie. Not his Eddie. This is… whoever this is, is not Eddie. Eddie is full of life. Eddie is warmth and sunlight and tanned skin. Eddie is flushed cheeks, a small blush working its way across his face at any compliment. Eddie is sparkling, shining eyes, crinkling around the corners, watching Buck inevitably make a fool of himself. That is Eddie.Or, what if the elevator opened and Eddie didn't wake up? Sort of 9x18 fix it (depends on what you define as fixing) for Buck’s reaction and the aftermath.

entry.title: get a grip
entry.summary:
Author: honey_coffee_and_vanilla

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,234Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 43

Summary: He paused, wondering if he should just deny the whole thing and make a comment about the sport that he couldn't remember the name of. But instead, he set his beer bottle down on the coffee table. It had about a quarter left. Eddie was sure he must have been the one drinking it, but he once again couldn't recall. It must have gotten lost in the foot tap, Buck's stretching of his muscles as he slapped the bottle caps off, and the movement of his Adam's apple while he sipped his own beer.---Buck and Eddie are friends. Friends have a couple beers, watch the game, and talk about their feelings. Friends do not start making out and grabbing each other's biceps. Maybe Buck and Eddie aren't friends.

entry.title: Second Chances
entry.summary:
Author: starylighting

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 855,121Chapters: 87/?Kudos: 126

Summary: Bobby and Athena are just two people trying to put their lives back together while figuring out how to co-parent. What starts as a complicated plan to foster kids in need slowly turns into something much deeper. Through the ups and downs of heartbreak, healing, and raising children who’ve been through so much, they find strength in each other. Over time, their partnership grows into love, and together, they build a story about family, trust, and second chances.

entry.title: Evergrowing
entry.summary:
Author: starylighting

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 626,377Chapters: 80/?Kudos: 34

Summary: Athena and Bobby are learning that parenting doesn’t end when the kids turn eighteen. With three grown children and two still at home, Bobby and Athena find themselves navigating new challenges at every stage of life. Each child carries their own struggles, some spoken and some hidden, but together the family works through the ups and downs with love, patience, and resilience. At the heart of it all, they’re reminded that family is a bond that grows, shifts, and deepens, no matter how old the kids get.

entry.title: Your Heart’s a Bird Without the Wings to Fly
entry.summary:
Author: catniss_37

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 18,685Chapters: 6/19Kudos: 89

Summary: "Buck!" Eddies voice rang through the space, abruptly cut off by the deafening sound of crumpling concrete hitting the ground. Eddie stared at the rubble now blocking the way between himself and Buck. "Buck," Eddie whimpered as he dropped to his knees in front of the pile of rubble Buck was buried under. OR If episode 9x3 actually included the subway collapse from the promo, because I'm so mad about that.Continuing the story using that as a plot point. It will be canon divergent from that point onward in season 9, episode 3.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: but i’m tied tight (cut me loose)
entry.summary:
Author: hereforthevibess

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,549Chapters: 1/3Kudos: 11

Summary: Occasionally Eddie will wonder what even drew Shannon to him in the first place. And he tries hard not to bring it up, the last thing he wants was to reek of insecurity — especially after five years. But it rattled around in his mind from time to time, growing louder at times like these. When she was the life of the party. It meant all of Eddie’s friends were more Shannon’s friends, which — well, felt a little pathetic. If they ever broke up, it was a no-brainer who would win custody. It was obvious who would be left alone. Except Buck. No — Buck was all Eddie’s. — Or; Eddie tries to navigate the complexities of his two most important relationships in college —Buck and Shannon.
